briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 28, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 The Reality of Climate Change: Rising Threats and Perceived Impact
sections.content: According to recent surveys, 57.5% of domestic respondents report directly experiencing the impacts of climate change, with negative emotions such as stress and sadness becoming more widespread [1]. Internationally, extreme weather phenomena are intensifying due to climate change, including an increase in eastern wildfires such as those in Georgia [58, 60], and the strengthening of the most powerful hurricanes [64]. Furthermore, threats to biodiversity and vulnerable populations are growing, such as the extinction threat to Hadzabe hunter-gatherers [66] and the disproportionate impact on women in South Africa [73]. Research and warnings continue regarding the overall impact on health and ecosystems, including maternal health [57], setbacks in malaria eradication efforts [69], and an increase in rodent populations [68] [59, 67].

sections.title: ⚠️ Policy and Institutional Challenges: Efforts and Limitations in Achieving Carbon Neutrality Goals
sections.content: Domestically, the low enrollment rate in Incheon's carbon neutrality point system indicates difficulties in encouraging citizen participation [9, 12]. Furthermore, concerns about policy effectiveness are deepening as arguments are made for the need to legislate long-term reduction pathways to align the current Carbon Neutrality Framework Act with the 1.5-degree global temperature rise target [10]. Internationally, political conflicts surrounding climate change response persist, with ongoing criticism of budget proposals described as 'climate change denialist' [52] and protest activities criticizing climate setbacks [75]. Meanwhile, research indicating that climate change increases threats to democracy [62], and the observed downgrading of forests within environmental restructuring processes, along with the removal of certain climate change-related areas, are raising concerns [71].

sections.title: 🌍 International Cooperation and Awareness Promotion: Solidarity for Climate Change Response
sections.content: International cooperation and efforts to raise awareness for climate change response are also actively underway. Minister Cho Hyun met with the Prime Minister of Bhutan to discuss cooperation measures in various fields, including climate change, strengthening solidarity between the two nations [2, 4, 7]. King Charles III of the UK is scheduled to discuss climate change during his visit to the United States, despite differing views with former President Trump, highlighting the sustained interest from high-level figures [54]. Furthermore, public awareness is growing, with readers in Oregon, USA, expressing the view that climate change is an undeniable fact [55], and NATO supporting climate change research and talent development through its 2026 Climate Change and Security Centre of Excellence Fellowship Program [72]. SCB-Africa is encouraging the role of future generations by emphasizing teamwork and innovation among young scientists to combat climate change [74].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Efforts and Practices: Participation and Innovation by Diverse Stakeholders
sections.content: Domestically, various stakeholders continue their efforts to address climate change. Adaptation in the agricultural sector is progressing, with 'national fruit' apples seeking new strategies to cope with climate change [3, 5]. Regional innovations are also being attempted, such as one area pursuing a major transformation from an 'isolated island' to a 'hyper-connected carbon-neutral city' [8]. KEPCO E&C is achieving carbon neutrality through renewable energy and future nuclear power technologies [14], while the pharmaceutical and bio industries and companies are leading ESG management practices for carbon neutrality [11, 20]. Furthermore, policy efforts to encourage citizen participation are underway, including Representative Wi Seong-gon's proposal of legislation to mandate cash incentives for carbon neutrality practices and link them with local currencies [13, 23]. The Ministry of Climate has launched the 'Republic of Korea Climate Action Preparatory Committee' [28], and local governments, civic groups, and educational institutions are actively engaging in climate action through operating carbon neutrality promotional booths [24], environmental cleanup activities [25, 26, 27], workshops [15], citizen participation events [17], and experiential environmental education [29]. Artificial Intelligence (AI) is also being utilized as an innovative solution, supporting convergent research on climate change [56] and achieving carbon neutrality through green data centers [22].

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Acceleration of Future Energy Transition
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is spreading as a key driving force in global climate change response. Korea Water Resources Corporation (K-water) is taking proactive steps to expand renewable energy projects, holding cooperation meetings with local governments and forming an 'energy alliance' with seven municipalities [30, 37, 39, 43, 49]. Korea East-West Power (EWP) is seeing large-scale renewable energy projects come to fruition, including the completion of Gyeonggi Province's largest 'direct transaction' solar power plant [32, 40, 42, 46, 47, 48]. Korea Western Power (WP) is pursuing long-term investments and plans, setting a target of 13.9 GW of renewable energy by 2040 and expanding its organization [36]. Internationally, the use of renewable energy is expanding across various industrial sectors, with PepsiCo leading renewable energy agreements with Spanish suppliers [38], and Soluna Holdings launching a renewable energy-based Bitcoin mining operation in Texas [41]. Despite the Iran crisis, a 'significant return to coal' is not expected globally in 2026 [77]. Furthermore, despite former President Trump's attempts to hinder clean energy development, clean energy is now out-producing fossil fuels, demonstrating that the transition to renewable energy is an irreversible trend [76].

briefing.closing: As we have reviewed in today's briefing, the threat of climate change is becoming an increasingly tangible reality, yet various domestic and international efforts to overcome it are actively underway. Sustained policy improvements, technological innovation, and active participation from civil society will be key to overcoming the climate crisis. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: The labor sector calls for 'structural reforms, including limiting the reasons for using fixed-term contracts.' The government has officially introduced a fair wage system to improve the treatment of irregular workers in the public sector, but faces criticism for lacking key measures to prevent the abuse of fixed-term contracts.

summary.phenomenon: The government has officially announced the introduction of a fair wage system to improve the treatment of irregular workers in the public sector.
summary.outlook: The labor sector criticized that the absence of key measures to reduce the abuse of fixed-term contracts could render the initiative a 'storm in a teacup,' advocating for structural reforms such as limiting the reasons for their use.

articles.original_description: 노동계 “기간제 사용사유 제한 등 구조개혁 필요”이재명 대통령이 28일 청와대에서 열린 국무회의 겸 비상경제점검회의에서 발언하고 있다. 청와대사진기자단정부가 공공부문 비정규직 처우 개선 대책으로 공정수당 도입을 공식화했지만, 기간제 남용을 줄이기 위한 핵심 대책이 빠졌다는 비판이 나온다. 노동계는 기간제 사용을 예외적으로만 허용하는 ‘사용 사유 제한’ 법제···

articles.title: Samyang Group's 'Sudang Award' Selects Professors Hwang Il-doo and Cho Sung-bae
articles.original_title: 삼양그룹 ‘수당상’에 황일두·조성배 교수 선정
articles.description: The Sudang Foundation, Samyang Group's scholarship foundation, announced on the 28th that it has selected Professor Hwang Il-doo, Seokcheon Chair Professor of Life Sciences at POSTECH (left photo), and Professor Cho Sung-bae of Computer Science at Yonsei University (right) as recipients of the 35th Sudang Award. Professor Hwang, the recipient in the basic science category, was recognized for elucidating the developmental signal transduction system and the evolutionary principles of vascular bundles, which play a key role in plant biomass production and eco-friendly crop development for climate change response, significantly contributing to domestic botany.

summary.phenomenon: Samyang Group's Sudang Foundation announced on the 28th that Professor Hwang Il-doo of POSTECH's Department of Life Sciences has been selected as a recipient of the 35th Sudang Award.
summary.cause: Professor Hwang was recognized for his contributions in elucidating the developmental signal transduction system and the evolutionary principles of vascular bundles, which play a crucial role in plant biomass production and eco-friendly crop development for climate change response.

articles.original_description: 삼양그룹 장학재단인 수당재단이 제35회 수당상 수상자로 황일두 포항공대 생명과학과 석천석좌교수(왼쪽 사진)와 조성배 연세대 컴퓨터과학과 교수(오른쪽)를 선정했다고 28일 밝혔다.기초과학 부문 수상자인 황 교수는 기후변화 대응을 위한 식물 바이오매스 생산과 친환경 작물 개발에 핵심 역할을 하는 발달 신호 전달 체계와 관다발의 진화 원리를 규명해 국내 식물학 ···

articles.original_title: Singing activists disrupt NatWest meeting over ‘climate backtracking’
articles.description: AGM briefly adjourned after protesters wearing T-shirts labelled ‘No more big oil’ burst into songThe chair of NatWest was forced to defend the bank against accusations of “climate backtracking” at a chaotic annual shareholder meeting, which was temporarily suspended owing to singing protesters.Not long after the meeting began in Edinburgh, it was adjourned for about half an hour after a protester interrupted Rick Haythornthwaite’s opening speech. Continue reading...

summary.phenomenon: At the NatWest Annual General Meeting held in Edinburgh, protesters wearing T-shirts labeled 'No more big oil' disrupted the meeting by singing, leading to a temporary suspension for about 30 minutes.
summary.cause: Protesters accused NatWest of 'climate backtracking' and expressed dissatisfaction with the bank's climate-related policies.

articles.original_title: Trump’s attempt to crush clean energy progress not going to plan, experts say
articles.description: US generated more power from renewables including solar and wind than gas last month in a firstDonald Trump has wielded the full might of his administration to crush the progress of clean energy, which he has called a “scam” and “stupid”. But there are signs this assault is not going to plan.In March, the US generated more of its electricity from renewable sources such as solar and wind than it did via gas, the first time clean energy has surpassed the planet-heating fossil fuel for a full month

summary.phenomenon: Last March, the United States generated more electricity from renewable sources such as solar and wind than from gas, marking the first time clean energy surpassed fossil fuels for a full month.
summary.cause: The increase in power generation from renewable sources is attributed to advancements in solar and wind power generation technologies and their widespread adoption, reaching a level that surpasses gas power generation.
summary.outlook: Experts suggest that former President Donald Trump's attempts to hinder clean energy development are not proceeding as planned, indicating continued growth for renewable energy.
